title: Bank Statement Analysis for Medical Spas and Aesthetic Practices
seo_title: Bank Statement Analysis for Medical Spas and Aesthetic
meta: Med spas have high cash-pay revenue and low insurance dependence. Learn how lenders analyze medical spa bank statements for equipment financing and business loans.
content:
Medical Spa Revenue: A Favorable Profile for Lenders

Medical spas and aesthetic practices (Botox, fillers, laser treatments, body contouring, skin care services) are among the most cash-flow-positive healthcare businesses. The reason: they operate almost exclusively on a cash-pay model. Unlike traditional medical practices dependent on insurance reimbursements, med spas collect payment at the time of service with minimal accounts receivable.

How Med Spa Revenue Appears on Statements

  • Card processing settlements: Daily deposits from Square, Stripe, or a spa-specific POS. High-ticket services (Botox, filler, laser) at $300–$3,000 per treatment create larger average transaction values than most retail businesses.
  • Membership program deposits: Many med spas operate monthly membership or treatment package programs — appearing as regular recurring ACH or card charges. This creates predictable recurring revenue.
  • Cash deposits: Some clients still pay cash; regular cash deposits are expected and normal.
  • Gift card / package sales: Higher during Q4 and around holidays (Mother's Day) — creating seasonal revenue spikes.

High-Ticket, Low-Volume Transactions

Unlike restaurants or retail where deposits are many small amounts, med spa card settlements are fewer but larger. A day with 8 clients might produce only 8 transactions but total $5,000 in deposits. This is a quality revenue signal — each transaction represents significant value delivered.

Equipment-Intensive Investment

Med spas require significant medical equipment investment: laser platforms ($50,000–$250,000), body contouring devices, microneedling tools, and injectables. Equipment financing appears as regular scheduled payments to medical equipment lenders — expected and positive for the business profile.

For businesses seeking additional equipment financing, bank statement analysis that shows consistent high-ticket service revenue helps lenders confidently underwrite equipment loans.

What Lenders Focus On

  • Average monthly deposits from card settlements and memberships
  • Membership/subscription revenue as a stability indicator
  • NSF events — virtually zero for a well-run cash-pay business
  • Equipment financing payments as normal capital deployment
  • Seasonal revenue spikes contextualized (not flagged as income manipulation)

Common Loan Products

  • Equipment financing for laser and aesthetic devices
  • Practice acquisition loans
  • Business expansion loans for second locations
  • Working capital lines for marketing and staffing during growth phases

Related: Dental practice bank statement analysis | Physician bank statement analysis

Bottom Line

Medical spas are excellent lending candidates. Cash-pay revenue, high-ticket transactions, and membership-based recurring income create strong, clean bank statement profiles. Equipment financing in this sector is particularly well-supported by bank statement cash flow analysis.
